Energy-Adjusted Metals Tailwind Signal
← Back to dashboard

Energy-Adjusted Metals Tailwind Signal

Energy- and FX-adjusted copper residual as a metals demand tailwind indicator.

As of Unknown

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • The Energy-Adjusted Metals Tailwind signal is currently in a strong Demand_Tailwind regime, indicating significant copper-specific demand exceeding that explained by oil and the U.S. dollar (1). The latest z-score of 3.03 on 2026-01-31 signifies robust expansion.
  • Conviction Band: Extreme | Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence | Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Key Dynamics:

  • Copper prices (COPPER_YF_USD_LB) are 6.0150, significantly higher than the oil and USD-fitted price of 3.414216 as of 2026-01-31.
  • The Copper_Residual stands at 2.600784, leading to a robustly standardized Metals_Tailwind z-score of 3.030361 (1).
  • This reflects strong underlying demand for copper, independent of broader energy prices or currency movements.
  • Conditional Invalidation: A sustained and significant increase in crude oil prices (DCOILWTICO) or strengthening of the U.S. dollar (DTWEXBGS) that causes the Copper_Residual to consistently turn negative.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued strong copper demand tailwind, supported by its resilience against energy and currency influences.
  • Upside secondary: Further acceleration in copper-specific demand, potentially driven by global industrial recovery.
  • Downside residual: A sharp reversal in global growth or commodity sentiment, leading to copper price weakness beyond what oil and USD explain.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) as it captures medium-term demand trends and their persistence.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High, due to the extreme conviction and high confidence in a key global demand indicator.

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension informed: Global demand, pricing, and economic growth (1)(2).
  • Cycle position: Suggests a mid-cycle expansion or re-acceleration in demand for industrial metals.
  • Typical interaction with other macro signals: Often correlates positively with global growth proxies, industrial production, and business confidence, while acting as a lead indicator for commodity-linked inflation.

Data & References:

  • COPPER_YF_USD_LB (Copper Prices), latest observation: 2026-01-31 (1).
  • DCOILWTICO (WTI Crude Oil Prices), latest observation: 2026-01-31 (1).
  • DTWEXBGS (Broad Trade Weighted US Dollar Index), latest observation: 2026-01-31 (1).
  • The `Metals_Tailwind` (z-score of copper residual) is most influential, driven by the divergence of actual copper prices from the fitted model.
  • 1–2 additional public datasets that would improve depth or reliability: Global Manufacturing PMI, industrial production indices from major economies.

Energy-Adjusted Metals Tailwind Chart

Copper residual after controlling for WTI and the broad USD, scaled as a metals tailwind index.
